summary: Arqel dogfood loop — Round 13: re-detect against main (75 framework bugs fixed across rounds 0-12). Deepest probes into the least-covered surfaces (audit attribute filtering, realtime broadcast dispatch, mcp tools, cli generators, export xlsx/formatting, i18n/locale, theme, command palette, a11y, edge-case interactions between traits). Seen-filtered (81 sigs), adversarial verify. Round 12 found 2 (audit read-endpoint authz, table canSee dead) so the consecutive-clean counter is 0/2. Stop after 2 consecutive clean rounds.
logs: Round 13 detection: 5 from clusters = 5 after dedup/seen-filter
Round 13 verify: 5 confirmed NEW framework bugs, 0 app-misuse, 0 not-a-bug

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Versionable snapshot stores RAW (pre-cast) attribute values, so restoreToVersion() double-encodes any cast attribute (array/json/object/encrypted) — corrupting it permanently — and the version diff is type-asymmetric
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Make snapshot/restore cast-aware: build `payload` from cast values (e.g. `$model->attributesToArray()` or per-key `getAttribute()`/`castAttribute`) instead of raw `getAttributes()`; in `restoreToVersion` use `fill()`/`setAttribute` (re-applies casts) instead of `forceFill` of raw serialized strings; build the `updating` diff from cast values on BOTH sides so before/after are the same type.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced end-to-end against a casted model (mirroring apps/showcase Post `meta => array`) using the versioning package's own Orchestra testbench harness. All three facets confirmed concretely: (1) `writeVersion` uses `$model->getAttributes()` (raw), so `version.payload['meta']` is stored as the JSON STRING `'{"restore_test":true,"depth":1}'` (gettype = string), not an array. (2) `restoreToVersion` does `forceFill($payload)` + `save()`, feeding that JSON string into the array-cast attribute; Eloquent re-serializes it, so the raw DB value becomes the DOUBLE-ENCODED `'"{\"restore_test\":true,\"depth\":1}"'` and `$fresh->meta` reads back as a STRING — permanent silent corruption; `$fresh->meta['restore_test']` is broken. (3) The `updating` diff mixes cast-applied `getOriginal()` (array) with raw `getDirty()` (string), producing `changes.meta = [array, string]` — type-asymmetric, passed straight to the UI by VersionPresenter. The Version model casts payload/changes to array (JSON column) but that does not undo the corruption since the stored value is already a serialized string. Framework-side: nothing the app can configure away; the Article test fixture has no casts so the round-trip is unguarded, and SKILL.md only warns about PII, never the cast double-encoding. Severity high: silent permanent data corruption on restore of any array/json/object/collection/encrypted cast, hitting the exact showcase Post model.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: Versionable::writeVersion (packages/versioning/src/Concerns/Versionable.php:98-116) snapshots the payload via `$model->getAttributes()`, which returns the RAW DB-bound values (not cast). For a model with `protected $casts = ['meta' => 'array']` (exactly apps/showcase Post, which uses Versionable + LogsActivity + BelongsToTenant), the stored `payload.meta` is a JSON STRING, not an array. restoreToVersion() (Versionable.php:159-183) then does `forceFill($payload)` + `save()`, putting that JSON string back into the array-cast attribute; Eloquent re-encodes it on save, DOUBLE-ENCODING it. Live repro on showcase Post: set meta=['restore_test'=>true,'depth'=>1], version it (payload.meta stored as string "{\"restore_test\":true,\"depth\":1}"), change meta, then restoreToVersion(snapshot) -> RAW DB value becomes the double-encoded string `"{\"restore_test\":true,\"depth\":1}"` and `$fresh->meta` reads back as a STRING, not an array — `$fresh->meta['restore_test']` is broken; the attribute is permanently corrupted for every array/json/object/collection/encrypted cast. SECOND facet (same root cause): the `changes` diff is type-asymmetric because the `updating` hook (Versionable.php:54-70) builds the diff from `getOriginal($key)` (cast-applied -> array) vs `getDirty()` (raw -> JSON string). Live: editing meta produced `changes.meta = [{"hello":"world","k":1} (array), "{\"hello\":\"changed\"}" (string)]` — the before-side is a decoded array, the after-side is an escaped JSON string. VersionPresenter::toArray (packages/versioning/src/VersionPresenter.php:48-55) passes `changes` straight to the UI, so the history diff renders an array on one side and escaped JSON on the other. NOT covered by tests: the test fixture tests/Fixtures/Article.php declares no casted attributes, so the entire cast round-trip is unguarded. SKILL.md only warns the payload 'pode conter PII e segredos' — it never documents the cast double-encoding / restore corruption.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Command-palette navigation URLs hardcode '/admin', ignoring the configurable panel path (Panel::path / config('arqel.path'))
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In buildCommand(), replace the literal `url: '/admin/'.$slug` with a resolved panel-path prefix mirroring InertiaDataBuilder::resolvePanelPath(): compute `$path = '/'.trim(app(PanelRegistry::class)->getCurrent()?->getPath() ?? (is_string(config('arqel.path','admin')) ? config('arqel.path','admin') : 'admin'), '/')` (resolve once in provide() or a private helper), then `url: $path.'/'.$slug`. Update NavigationCommandProviderTest to cover a non-default path (e.g. config(['arqel.path' => 'manage']) asserts '/manage/users').
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced at the code level and traced end-to-end; could not refute. (1) NavigationCommandProvider.php:107 hardcodes `url: '/admin/'.$slug` with no read of Panel::getPath() or config('arqel.path'); the provider ctor only takes ResourceRegistry. (2) The real resource index route is registered as `Route::get('{resource}', ...)` (routes/arqel.php:30) under `Route::prefix($path)` where `$path = $panel?->getPath() ?? config('arqel.path','admin')` (ArqelServiceProvider.php:299-322). So a panel mounted via the public, user-facing builder Panel::path('/manage') (Panel.php) or config(['arqel.path'=>'backoffice']) serves the index at /manage/{slug}, not /admin/{slug}. (3) The JS consumer CommandPalette.tsx:219 does `window.location.assign(command.url)` verbatim — no client-side rewrite — so the hardcoded /admin/{slug} is navigated to as-is → 404 on a non-default path. (4) Every OTHER navigation surface resolves the configurable path: InertiaDataBuilder::resolvePanelPath() (lines 374-381) does the exact resolution, and its doc-comment (369) literally states the intent is to build absolute URLs 'without hardcoding /admin' — the palette is the lone surface that violates this. (5) The unit test only asserts the default '/admin/...' (lines 31,38,56), masking the gap (tests-mask-integration-gaps). This is the brief's 'documented config/builder never consumed at the real entry point' meta-class. Not a deferred feature and not intended behavior — Panel::path() is a shipped public builder consumed correctly everywhere else. Severity medium: default /admin works (most apps unaffected), sidebar/direct nav still function on non-default paths; only the secondary command-palette 'Go to' shortcut 404s, and the fix is local with an existing in-repo pattern to copy.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: packages/core/src/CommandPalette/Providers/NavigationCommandProvider.php:107 builds every navigation Command as `url: '/admin/'.$slug` (hardcoded). Every OTHER navigation surface resolves the configurable panel path: ArqelServiceProvider::registerResourceRoutes (src/ArqelServiceProvider.php:300-322) mounts the resource routes under `$panel?->getPath() ?? config('arqel.path','admin')` via `Route::prefix($path)`; the sidebar/edit/create payloads use InertiaDataBuilder::resolvePanelPath() (src/Support/InertiaDataBuilder.php:374-381), whose own doc-comment (line 369) states the intent is to 'construct absolute URLs without hardcoding /admin'. Panel::path(string) (src/Panel/Panel.php:179) is a public, user-facing builder, default '/admin' (Panel.php:24). Repro: a panel mounted at a non-default path — Panel::make()->path('/manage'), or config(['arqel.path' => 'backoffice']) — registers the resource index route at /manage/{slug}, the sidebar links to /manage/{slug}, but every command-palette 'Go to {Resource}' entry still points at /admin/{slug} → 404. The unit test tests/Unit/CommandPalette/Providers/NavigationCommandProviderTest.php:31,38,56 only asserts the DEFAULT '/admin/...' URLs, so it masks the non-default-path gap (tests-mask-integration-gaps). This is the brief's 'documented config/builder never consumed at the real entry point' meta-class: NavigationCommandProvider never reads getPath()/config('arqel.path'). Fix: resolve the panel path like resolvePanelPath() and prefix with it instead of the literal '/admin/'.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Dashboard SelectFilter::multiple() is a dead builder — DashboardFilters.tsx ignores the `multiple` flag and always renders a single-select
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In SelectControl, branch on filter.multiple: when true, render <select multiple> with array value (value={arr.map(String)}) and an onChange that emits Array.from(e.target.selectedOptions, o => o.value) (mirror TableFilters.tsx MultiSelectFilter lines 123-153); add a test in DashboardFilters.test that multiple:true renders a multi-select.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced on current main. PHP SelectFilter::multiple() (packages/widgets/src/Filters/SelectFilter.php:36-41) sets $this->multiple and getTypeSpecificProps() (lines 54-57) serializes 'multiple' => $this->multiple into the payload; SKILL.md:39 documents multiple(bool=true) as a public builder; FilterTest.php confirms multiple:true reaches toArray(). The React consumer DashboardFilters.tsx declares multiple?: boolean (line 18) but SelectControl (lines 100-128) renders a single <select> with no multiple attribute, scalar value={String(value)}, and single-string onChange — grep confirms line 18 is the ONLY occurrence of 'multiple' in widgets/, so the flag is read nowhere. The sibling table renderer TableFilters.tsx has a working MultiSelectFilter (lines 123-153) that renders <select multiple> with array value/onChange, proving multi-select is a real implemented capability elsewhere and the widgets renderer is inconsistent, not deliberately single-only. The component docstring ('single dropdown') describes the current broken state, not an intended scope, since the PHP builder + its docs + its serialization all promise multi-select. Concrete repro: SelectFilter::make('status')->multiple() serializes multiple:true, which reaches the component and is silently discarded, dropping the advertised multi-select. Same meta-class as prior confirmed fixes (documented/serialized builder never consumed at the real entry point), distinct entry point. Not crash-level: silent capability loss affecting only dashboards that call ->multiple(), hence medium.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: PHP `Arqel\Widgets\Filters\SelectFilter` exposes a public builder `multiple(bool $multiple = true)` and serializes it via getTypeSpecificProps() as `'multiple' => $this->multiple` (packages/widgets/src/Filters/SelectFilter.php:36-41,54-57). The React consumer `packages-js/ui/src/widgets/DashboardFilters.tsx` declares `multiple?: boolean` on `SelectFilterPayload` (line 18) but NEVER consumes it: `SelectControl` (lines 100-128) renders a plain single `<select>` with no `multiple` attribute, a scalar `value={String(value)}`, and an onChange that emits a single string (`e.target.value`). `grep -rn multiple packages-js/ui/src/widgets/` returns ONLY the type-declaration line — zero logic uses it, and no test references it. So a dashboard built with `SelectFilter::make('status')->multiple()` silently renders as a single-select, dropping the multi-select capability the PHP API advertises and serializes. This is provably the broken side: the analogous TABLE filter renderer `packages-js/ui/src/table/TableFilters.tsx:137` DOES honor `multiple` (renders a multi-select), so the widgets renderer is inconsistent, not intentionally single-only. Same meta-class as prior fixes (#139/#153 authGuard, RelationshipColumn JOIN): a documented/serialized builder method never consumed at the real entry point — but a DISTINCT entry point (dashboard filter control) not previously covered, with a concrete repro (the serialized `multiple:true` reaches the component and is discarded). Not in dogfood-seen.json (only `fields::multiple-filefield-no-element-rules`, an unrelated file-upload fix).

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: RecordActivityController ignores morph maps — empty/400 timeline when Relation::enforceMorphMap is active
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Resolve the stored morph value before querying: if class_exists($subjectType), query where('subject_type', (new $subjectType)->getMorphClass()); also accept morph aliases via Relation::getMorphedModel($subjectType) so a value emitted by GlobalActivityLogController (which serialises the stored getMorphClass() value) round-trips. Mirror the getMorphClass()-on-both-sides pattern already used in packages/workflow/src/Listeners/PersistStateTransitionToHistory.php and StateTransitionField.php (Round-10).
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Premise verified. Spatie writes subject_type via subject()->associate($model) (vendor ActivityLogger.php:50), which stores $model->getMorphClass() — the morph-map ALIAS when Relation::enforceMorphMap() is active. RecordActivityController::show() line 41 hard-requires class_exists($subjectType) (so an alias like 'post' returns HTTP 400 invalid_subject_type) and line 59 queries where('subject_type', $subjectType) with the raw FQCN (so when storage holds the alias, the query returns ZERO rows -> empty timeline for a record that has activity). GlobalActivityLogController::serialiseActivity() emits the stored value (the alias) as subject_type, so a client drilling from the global feed into the per-record endpoint passes the alias and hits the 400. grep confirms 0 getMorphClass/enforceMorphMap hits in packages/audit/src and no morph-map test in packages/audit/tests. The sibling workflow package (Round-10) already uses getMorphClass() on BOTH write and read sides, proving the framework's own established morph-safe pattern, which audit deviates from. This is a distinct entry point/package from the Round-10 workflow fix. NOT documented intended behavior: the controller docblock asserts the FQCN 'matches what spatie stores', a false invariant under morph maps — it does not scope morph-map support out. HOWEVER: the live failure requires Relation::enforceMorphMap() to be active, and on current main NO framework source and NO showcase app config enforces a morph map (it appears only in two unrelated test files in versioning/workflow). So against the apps/showcase baseline on main this is latent/conditional, not currently firing — hence low severity. It is a genuine correctness defect for the standard, recommended Laravel practice of enforcing a morph map, which a framework-provided activity-log endpoint should support.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: packages/audit/src/Http/Controllers/RecordActivityController.php::show() requires class_exists($subjectType) (line 41) and queries Activity::query()->where('subject_type', $subjectType) with that FQCN (line 59). But spatie/laravel-activitylog stores subject_type via $subject->getMorphClass() (packages/audit/vendor/spatie/laravel-activitylog/src/Models/Activity.php:121, and the morphTo relation), which returns the MORPH-MAP ALIAS (e.g. 'post') when Relation::enforceMorphMap() is set. Two distinct live failures result: (1) the producer side — GlobalActivityLogController/RecordActivityController serialize subject_type as the stored alias 'post', so a client drilling in calls the endpoint with 'post' -> class_exists('post') is false -> HTTP 400 invalid_subject_type; (2) if the client instead passes the FQCN, line 59 filters where('subject_type','App\Models\Post') while rows hold 'post' -> ZERO results -> empty activity timeline for a record that has activity. Spatie's own forSubject() scope uses getMorphClass(); the controller does not. No morph handling anywhere in packages/audit/src (grep getMorphClass/enforceMorphMap = 0 hits) and no morph-map test in packages/audit/tests. Distinct entry point from the Round-10 workflow stateTransitions() morph fix (different package/path). Fix: resolve query class via the subject's getMorphClass() (and accept aliases via Relation::getMorphedModel()) instead of the raw FQCN.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Password reset / forgot-password flow hardwired to the default 'users' broker — ignores Panel::authGuard provider
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Add a `resolvePasswordBroker()` helper (parallel to ResolvesPanelGuard) that maps the panel guard's provider to its broker name and pass it: `Password::broker($this->resolvePasswordBroker())` in both forgot/reset controllers; make Panel::passwordResetExpirationMinutes() write `auth.passwords.{broker}.expire` for the panel's broker instead of hardcoded `users`; and have RegisterRequest::resolveUsersTable()/email-unique check read the panel-guard provider model rather than `auth.providers.users.model`.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced in source on current main. ForgotPasswordController::__invoke (line 39) and ResetPasswordController::__invoke (line 37) both call Password::broker() with no argument, so they always resolve the default 'users' broker regardless of the panel's configured guard. Confirmed the divergence: RegisterController::resolveUserModel() (lines 74-83) explicitly derives the Eloquent model from the panel guard's provider via ResolvesPanelGuard, and Login/Logout/EmailVerification/EnsureUserCanAccessPanel all use ResolvesPanelGuard too — but the password-reset path does not. Panel::passwordResetExpirationMinutes() (Panel.php:366) hardcodes config(['auth.passwords.users.expire' => ...]), and RegisterRequest::resolveUsersTable() (line 69) hardcodes auth.providers.users.model for the email-unique check while the controller creates in the guard's model. So a panel using ->authGuard('admins') with a non-'users' provider would register+login+verify against the right model but its forgot/reset links would target the wrong provider and never resolve, and the expire knob would mutate the wrong broker. The #139/#153 guard sweep (ResolvesPanelGuard, PanelAuthGuardTest, EmailVerificationGuardTest) verifiably never touched the password broker path. This is a real framework-internal inconsistency, not documented/intended behavior or a deferred feature. Severity is low: the bug is latent — it only manifests when a panel is configured with a guard whose provider is not 'users', which the showcase baseline does NOT do (config/auth.php uses web→users), so it is not reproducible at runtime in showcase as shipped; and it fails closed (reset links simply don't resolve) rather than opening a security hole.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: ForgotPasswordController::__invoke (packages/auth/src/Http/Controllers/ForgotPasswordController.php:39) and ResetPasswordController::__invoke (packages/auth/src/Http/Controllers/ResetPasswordController.php:37) both call Password::broker() with no argument -> always the default 'users' broker. Panel::passwordResetExpirationMinutes() (packages/core/src/Panel/Panel.php:366) likewise writes config(['auth.passwords.users.expire' => ...]) hardcoding 'users'. Meanwhile RegisterController::resolveUserModel() and the rest of the bundled auth flow honor Panel::authGuard(...) via ResolvesPanelGuard (the #139/#153 sweep). So a panel configured with a custom guard whose provider is NOT 'users' (e.g. an 'admins' provider/model/table) can register + log in + verify against the right guard, but forgot/reset-password silently target the wrong user provider — reset links never resolve the panel's users, and the expire-minutes knob mutates the wrong broker config. The #139/#153 guard sweep touched guard resolution but never the password broker, so this is a distinct, untouched code path. Secondary corroboration: RegisterRequest::resolveUsersTable() (packages/auth/src/Http/Requests/RegisterRequest.php:67-85) hardcodes config('auth.providers.users.model') for the email-unique check while RegisterController creates in the panel-guard's model — same divergence in the validation layer.
